notes: mistral-vibe `!`-shell escape. vibe runs `!echo hello` LOCALLY (no LLM turn) and writes ONE injected user line to messages.jsonl: `{"role":"user","injected":true,"content":"Manual `!` command result from the user. Use this as context only...."}` (confirmed in 3 live transcripts; source app.py:1574-1580 + agent_loop.py:751-777). As of fix 5e0b4f5c the vibe parser SKIPS injected:true user lines (parser.go:59-71: `if injected { ev.Skip=true; return ev }`), so the injected `!` result is NOT treated as activity: state stays ready throughout the shell-escape window and the session no longer sticks in working. This meets the scenario's 'state does not stick in working' / step-5 'returns to ready'. The final real prompt (injected:false) drives the ordinary ready→working→ready arc, proving the session is still alive. Single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<ts>_<hash> transcript directory via SessionIDFromPath); session_birth anchored to start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal the birth; record settles the presession-vs-real ordering. Observations: agent=mistral-vibe; model=mistral-medium-3.5 (confirmed from live meta.json config.active_model, passes NormalizeModelName unchanged); tokens_nonzero=true (sidecar stats.context_tokens on turn_done); cost_nonzero=false (adapter reads no session_cost; mistral-medium-3.5 absent from core/pkg/capacity). Driver: gap:wait_turn (baseline column gap — record ports SEAMs 1/2/3 before driving).

text: vibe session appears in ready at launch (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session_<ts>_<hash> id, whichever arrives first; single-birth, anchored UNPINNED to start)

After `!echo hello` runs locally, state stays ready — the injected `!` result is context-only and the parser skips it (parser.go:59-71, fix 5e0b4f5c), so no turn is synthesized and the session does not stick in working.
After the shell-escape window, a normal user prompt ('Reply with exactly the word: alive') drives ready → working — proves the session is still alive
Final turn completes back to ready (assistant terminal text → turn_done → ready)
